title: subscription-instance model tracks active customer subscriptions with lifecycle state
content:
# subscription-instance model tracks active customer subscriptions with lifecycle state

`powerhouse/subscription-instance` represents an active customer subscription. It is initialized from a ServiceOffering's final configuration.

## State Shape

```graphql
type SubscriptionInstanceState {
    customerId: PHID               # → customer identity (external)
    customerName: String
    customerEmail: EmailAddress
    customerType: CustomerType     # INDIVIDUAL | TEAM
    operatorId: PHID               # → BuilderProfile (external)
    serviceOfferingId: PHID        # → ServiceOffering
    tierName: String
    selectedBillingCycle: BillingCycle
    resource: ResourceDocument     # { id: PHID!, label, thumbnailUrl }
    status: SubscriptionStatus!    # PENDING | ACTIVE | PAUSED | EXPIRING | CANCELLED
    services: [Service!]!
    serviceGroups: [ServiceGroup!]!
    # ... lifecycle timestamps, billing projections, budget, autoRenew
}
```

## Modules (5)

| Module | Operations | Purpose |
|--------|-----------|---------|
| subscription | 16 | Initialize, status transitions, customer info, tier info, billing projections, renewal |
| service | 9 | Add/remove services, setup/recurring costs, facet selections |
| service-group | 5 | Add/remove groups, add/remove services to groups, group costs |
| metrics | 5 | Add/update/remove service metrics, increment/decrement usage |
| customer | 2 | Set customer type, update team member count |

## Key References

- `serviceOfferingId: PHID` → ServiceOffering (source offering)
- `operatorId: PHID` → BuilderProfile (external)
- `customerId: PHID` → customer identity (external)
- `resource.id: PHID` → ResourceInstance (via ResourceDocument)
- Services/groups mirror ServiceOffering structure but with billing actuals

## Data Flow

ServiceOffering → (mapOfferingToSubscription) → SubscriptionInstance initialization. Since [[mapOfferingToSubscription transforms service offering state into subscription initialization]], this is the primary cross-model data flow.

---

description: 39 operations across 5 modules — manages customer subscriptions, billing, service groups, metrics, and lifecycle transitions (PENDING → ACTIVE → PAUSED → CANCELLED)
